display.purpose: The sign's message — Overpass 800, white on a signal slab.

prose.purpose: Newsreader serif for any sentence that explains, reassures, or advises.

label.purpose: Overpass Mono uppercase kickers, tags, and every number (money, miles, dates).

shadows.name: card
shadows.purpose: Card at rest — ambient paper-on-concrete material.

shadows.name: raised
shadows.purpose: Hover lift on cards that navigate.

motion.name: ease-snap
motion.purpose: Quick — fold toggles and state flips.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one filled action per group — Guide-Sign Blue, sturdy and plain.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #1b5faa; color: #fff; border: none; border-radius: 10px; padding: 12px 18px; font-family: 'Overpass', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.95rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.008em; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.12s ease, filter 0.12s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #164e8c; } .ds-btn-primary:active { filter: brightness(0.92); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1b5faa; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Danger Button
components.description: Destructive confirm — Signal Red, same sturdy geometry.
components.css: .ds-btn-danger { background: #b3402e; color: #fff; border: none; border-radius: 10px; padding: 12px 18px; font-family: 'Overpass', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.95rem; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; transition: filter 0.12s ease; } .ds-btn-danger:hover { filter: brightness(1.06); } .ds-btn-danger:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #b3402e; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Sign-face white sheet on the concrete canvas.
components.html:
Service schedule

Personalized from consensus intervals and this vehicle's logged history.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid rgba(35,39,43,0.13); border-radius: 16px; padding: 16px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(35,39,43,0.05), 0 10px 26px -14px rgba(35,39,43,0.18); } .ds-card-title { margin: 0 0 6px; font-family: 'Overpass', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1.02rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.012em; color: #23272b; } .ds-card-body { margin: 0; font-family: 'Overpass', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.95rem; color: #6b7076; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Globally themed field — white, hairline, 10px radius, blue focus ring.
components.css: .ds-input { background: #ffffff; color: #23272b; border: 1px solid rgba(35,39,43,0.2); border-radius: 10px; height: 42px; padding: 0 12px; font-family: 'Overpass', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.95rem; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #6b7076; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1b5faa; outline-offset: 1px; border-color: #1b5faa; }

components.name: Kicker Label
components.description: Overpass Mono uppercase eyebrow — the data-spine voice.
components.css: .ds-kicker { font-family: 'Overpass Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.16em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #6b7076; }

components.name: Verdict Panel
components.description: The road sign itself — signal-color slab with tag, headline, and serif sentence. The signature component; never invent alternative status displays.
components.html:
Verdict · Fix now

Brake pads worn to the metal

Every drive makes the repair bigger, and stopping power is dropping.
components.css: .ds-verdict { background: #b3402e; color: #fff; border-radius: 16px; padding: 18px 20px; } .ds-verdict-tag { font-family: 'Overpass Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.2em; text-transform: uppercase; opacity: 0.85; margin-bottom: 6px; } .ds-verdict-headline { margin: 0 0 8px; font-family: 'Overpass', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 800; font-size: 24px; line-height: 1.1; letter-spacing: -0.015em; text-wrap: balance; } .ds-verdict-sentence { margin: 0; font-family: 'Newsreader', Georgia, serif; font-size: 15.5px; line-height: 1.45; opacity: 0.96; }

narrative.overview: Coast's interface is built on the visual grammar of US highway signage: one message per sign, instantly legible at a glance, calm authority with zero decoration. Verdict panels are literal signs — saturated signal-color slabs carrying one declarative headline in the signage face and one humane serif sentence beneath it. Everything else is the roadside: a daylight-concrete canvas, white sign-face cards, asphalt-ink text, and mono data set like mile markers.
keyCharacteristics: One verdict per surface, set as signage; depth lives one tap below.
Four-signal band vocabulary (fix-now red / book-soon amber / can-coast blue / all-clear green) is universal and never remapped.
Paper-on-concrete materiality: white cards, soft ambient shadows, hairline borders.
Three-voice typography: signage (Overpass), human explanation (Newsreader), data (Overpass Mono).
Matter-of-fact, sturdy controls — dependable over expressive.

rules.name: The Signal Budget Rule
rules.body: The four signal colors carry status meaning only. They are never decoration and never appear at full saturation on an inactive element. If a screen shows red, something is genuinely urgent.

rules.name: The Earned Green Rule
rules.body: All-clear green renders only when real logged history justifies it. Absence of data is never green.

rules.name: The Three Voices Rule
rules.body: Signage (Overpass), explanation (Newsreader serif), data (Overpass Mono) — every text element belongs to exactly one voice. Numbers never render in the serif; verdicts never render in the mono.

rules.name: The Ambient-Only Rule
rules.body: Shadows describe material, never importance. Urgency is carried exclusively by the signal colors — a fix-now verdict gets no extra shadow.

dos: Do lead every surface with one verdict in signage voice; put detail one tap below.
Do use the four-signal bands for all status everywhere — same hue, same meaning, no new status colors.
Do set explanatory sentences in Newsreader serif and all numbers in Overpass Mono with tabular figures.
Do keep controls sturdy and plain: solid fills, 10px radius, 42px touch targets, visible focus ring.
Do hold text contrast at WCAG 2.1 AA (≥4.5:1); Muted Slate (#6b7076) is the lightest allowed text color.
donts: Don't build "the generic SaaS dashboard" — no stat-tile grids, no gradients, no density for its own sake.
Don't add "parts-store / service-lane upsell energy" — no banners, badges, or urgency theater that monetizes uncertainty.
Don't be "the fear-mongering car app" — Signal Red only for genuinely-stop-now; never dramatize a book-soon into red.
Don't show all-clear green for a vehicle without real logged history (The Earned Green Rule).
Don't stack two solid-fill buttons of equal weight; one primary per group.
Don't warm the neutrals — the canvas is cool daylight concrete, not cream or parchment.
Don't use side-stripe borders, gradient text, or nested cards, ever.
